name: Potato Soup with Kale & Bacon
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel potatoes and put them in a pot with enough cold, salted water to cover. Bring covered pot to a boil.
2. Lower heat and uncover the pot. Simmer until potatoes are tender, about 25 minutes. Drain, reserving cooking liquid.
3. Mash potatoes until lumpy; they should not be smooth.
4. Cook bacon until crisp. Drain and chop. Pour off all but 2 tablespoons of bacon fat, reserving for later use.
5. Stem and chop kale. Mince garlic.
6. Cook half the chopped kale and garlic in the 2 tablespoons reserved bacon fat over medium heat until wilted, about 1 minute. Season with salt and pepper; transfer to a bowl. Repeat with remaining ingredients.
7. Return all ingredients to the frying pan and add heavy cream. Simmer over low heat until kale is tender, about 10 minutes.
8. Mince chives.
Recipe can be made up to this point several hours ahead. Cooking and Serving:
9. Combine potatoes with about 4 cups of reserved cooking liquid in a large pot. Add more liquid or water if needed for desired consistency. Stir in the kale mixture until heated through, then season to taste with salt and pepper.
10. Serve soup in bowls, sprinkled with chives and bacon crumbles.
